title: Coffee with Marek
description: You meet Marek at a coffee shop. After ordering, he helps you book a Foreign Police appointment.
context: You and Marek are now friends. You meet him at a coffee shop. First you order from the waitress, then Marek helps you book an appointment at the Foreign Police.

exchanges.speaker: waitress
exchanges.translation: Good day! What will you have?
exchanges.prompt: The waitress asks what you want. You want a coffee. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Dám si kávu, prosím. — correct. Dám si is the standard ordering phrase in any café or restaurant.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Dám si... + what you want is how you order. Save the payment phrases for after you've ordered.

exchanges.speaker: waitress
exchanges.text: Dobre. To je tri eurá päťdesiat. Platíte kartou alebo v hotovosti?
exchanges.translation: Good. That's three euros fifty. Do you pay by card or cash?
exchanges.prompt: The waitress asks whether you pay by card or cash. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct. Platím kartou means I'm paying by card; Platím v hotovosti means I'm paying in cash.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Platím kartou for card, or Platím v hotovosti for cash.

exchanges.speaker: waitress
exchanges.translation: Here you are. Do you want a receipt?
exchanges.prompt: The waitress asks if you want a receipt. What do you say?

choices.translation: Yes, can I get a receipt?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct. You can say Nie, ďakujem if you don't want a receipt, or Áno, môžem dostať účtenku? if you do.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Answer the receipt question directly: Nie, ďakujem or Áno, môžem dostať účtenku?

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Ahoj! Ako sa máš? Počul som, že potrebuješ pomoc s niečím na cudzineckej polícii.
exchanges.translation: Hi! How are you? I heard you need help with something at the Foreign Police.
exchanges.prompt: Marek greets you and mentions the Foreign Police. How do you respond?

choices.translation: Hi! Yes, I need to make an appointment.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Ahoj! Áno, potrebujem urobiť stretnutie. — exactly right. You greeted him back and confirmed what you need.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Ahoj! Áno, potrebujem urobiť stretnutie is the natural response — greeting him back and confirming the appointment need.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Dobre, pomôžem ti. Aký deň by si chcel ísť?
exchanges.translation: Good, I'll help you. What day would you like to go?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks what day works for you. Pick the day that suits you.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct pattern — [Day] by bol/bola dobrý/dobrá. Any day works, the grammar is what matters.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: [Day] by bol/bola dobrý/dobrá is the pattern for suggesting a day. Pick whichever day suits you.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Dobre, zapíšem to. A o koľkej by si chcel ísť?
exchanges.translation: Good, I'll note that down. And what time would you like to go?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks what time works for you. Pick a time.
exchanges.correctFeedback: O + time — correct pattern for saying 'at' a specific time. Pick whichever time suits you.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: O + time is the pattern. Any time works as long as the grammar is correct.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Super. A v ktorom mesiaci to potrebuješ vybaviť? Tvoj pobyt platí dokedy?
exchanges.translation: Great. And which month do you need to sort this out? Until when is your residence valid?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks which month your permit expires. Pick a month.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Platí do + month — correct pattern for saying when something expires. Any month works here.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Platí do + month is the pattern for expiry dates. Pick whichever month fits your situation.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Dobre, máme čas. Je to tvoja prvá návšteva na cudzineckej polícii?
exchanges.translation: Good, we have time. Is this your first visit to the Foreign Police?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if this is your first visit. What do you say if it IS your first time?

choices.text: Áno, je to moja prvá návšteva.
Áno, je to moja druhá návšteva.
Nie, som tu už desiaty raz.
choices.translation: No, I'm here for the tenth time already.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, je to moja prvá návšteva. — correct. Prvá = first, the ordinal you learned in the Numbers lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: If it's your first time, Áno, je to moja prvá návšteva is correct — prvá means first.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Rozumiem. Teraz mi povedz svoje číslo pasu. Začni prvým číslom.
exchanges.translation: I understand. Now tell me your passport number. Start with the first digit.
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for the first digit of your passport number. It's 5. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Päť. — correct, five. Reading numbers digit by digit is exactly how you'll give document numbers in Slovak.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Päť = five. When reading a document number aloud, say each digit using the cardinal numbers you learned.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Výborne, zapísal som všetko. Stretnutie je dohodnuté. Ešte niečo k káve?
exchanges.translation: Excellent, I noted everything down. The appointment is arranged. Anything else with the coffee?
exchanges.prompt: Marek finishes helping and asks if you need anything else. How do you thank him?

choices.translation: Thank you very much for the help!

exchanges.correctFeedback: Ďakujem veľmi pekne za pomoc! — perfect. A warm, genuine thank you for Marek's help with the appointment.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Ďakujem veľmi pekne za pomoc! is the right way to thank Marek warmly for everything he just helped with.

completionMessage: 🎉 Great job! You ordered coffee, paid, and worked with Marek to book your Foreign Police appointment — confirming the day, time, month, and even reading out your passport number. All in Slovak.
